title: Add PWA Install Button to App Header/UI

requirements.text: Add a prominent 'Install App' button in the application UI (e.g., in the Header component alongside existing navigation controls) that triggers the PWA installation prompt. The button should use the existing `pwaUtils.ts` install prompt utilities and the `PWAInstallPrompt.tsx` component logic. The button must: (1) only be visible when the app is not already running in standalone/installed mode, (2) handle iOS (show install instructions), Android, and desktop flows appropriately, (3) display a platform-appropriate label such as 'Install App' or 'Add to Home Screen', and (4) be styled consistently with the existing neon dark theme.

quotes: adicionar um botao no aplicativo para forçar a instalação como aplicativo pwa. Ainda nao estamos usando o aplicativo sem necessidade de browser.

acceptanceCriteria: An 'Install App' button is visible in the app UI when the app is not already installed/running in standalone mode.
Clicking the button on Android/Desktop triggers the native browser install prompt (beforeinstallprompt).
On iOS, clicking the button shows instructions for adding to home screen (since iOS does not support programmatic install prompts).
The button is hidden when the app is already running in standalone (installed) mode.
The button styling matches the existing neon dark theme used throughout the app.
The button is accessible and visible without requiring the user to scroll or navigate.

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths (useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, components/ui).
Reuse existing pwaUtils.ts and PWAInstallPrompt.tsx logic; do not duplicate installation detection or prompt management code.
nonGoals: Do not modify backend logic or Motoko files.
Do not add push notification or background sync features.
Do not change the existing PWA manifest or service worker configuration.
Do not add a new full-page onboarding flow — only a button/inline prompt.
